expectations.text: Explains the two distinct stages correctly: CERTIFICATION = technical assessment that NZISM controls are implemented and effective; ACCREDITATION = formal acceptance of residual risk by the Accreditation Authority allowing the system to operate
expectations.evidence: "Certification — the technical assessment confirming NZISM controls are implemented *and effective*... Accreditation — the formal acceptance of residual risk by the Accreditation Authority (typically the agency head or a delegate), granting Authorisation to Operate (ATO)."

expectations.text: Identifies the missing Security Risk Management Plan (SRMP) as a core gap — C&A requires documented risk assessment/management artifacts alongside the SSP/SecPlan
expectations.evidence: "Security Risk Management Plan | Mandatory C&A artefact... | Does not exist | Not Implemented — critical gap; certification cannot proceed without an SRMP"

expectations.text: Flags the absence of control validation testing as blocking certification (controls must be verified as implemented and effective, not just documented), and the missing ITSM/security-practitioner sign-off in the certification chain
expectations.evidence: "Control validation/testing | Independent technical testing evidence required — documentation alone is insufficient | No validation testing performed | Not Implemented — critical gap..." and "Certification review and sign-off... Not started (no ITSM sign-off)... Not Implemented — blocked by the SRMP and validation gaps above"

expectations.text: Correctly frames NZISM authorship/authority: issued by the GCSB (NCSC NZ) as the NZ government information security manual, with MUST/SHOULD compliance requirements tied to classification (RESTRICTED here), and notes essential/baseline controls cannot simply be waived without formal risk acceptance
expectations.evidence: "The NZISM is issued by the GCSB (National Cyber Security Centre — NCSC NZ)... Its controls carry MUST/SHOULD compliance requirements tied to the system's classification level — for a RESTRICTED system, essential (MUST) controls cannot be waived without formal risk acceptance recorded by the Accreditation Authority."

expectations.text: Produces a usable gap table/plan with prioritised remediation (SRMP first, control validation, certification review, then accreditation decision) rather than generic advice
expectations.evidence: Gap Analysis table plus "Recommended Remediation Priority" numbered list: 1. Commission the SRMP, 2. Complete control implementation, 3. Commission independent control validation/testing, 4. ITSM and CISO certification review, 5. Submit for accreditation decision
